perturbation.original_text: BII shall supply XENCOR with data, results and information required to comply with any mandatory request of any applicable regulatory body in the Major Territories to comply with such regulatory body's requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: BII will use reasonable efforts to supply XENCOR with available data, results, and information if formally requested and if such data is readily accessible, pertaining to regulatory compliance requirements in the Major Territories.
perturbation.explanation: The modification softens the obligation by replacing 'BII shall supply' with 'BII will use reasonable efforts to supply' and adds conditions such as 'if formally requested' and 'if such data is readily accessible.' This reduces the definiteness of BII's commitment to provide essential regulatory compliance information.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 21 CFR § 314.50 - Content and format of an application
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 314.50 outlines the content and format requirements for a New Drug Application (NDA) submitted to the FDA. This includes comprehensive data and information related to the drug's manufacturing, controls, and clinical trials. By introducing qualifiers such as 'reasonable efforts' and 'readily accessible,' the modified text creates uncertainty regarding the completeness and accessibility of the data provided by BII. This directly contradicts the requirement for a complete and well-organized application as mandated by 21 CFR § 314.50, potentially leading to delays or rejection of the NDA.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates BII to supply XENCOR with necessary data to comply with regulatory bodies. The changed text weakens this obligation by adding conditions ('reasonable efforts,' 'if formally requested,' 'readily accessible'). The Law Explanation highlights that 21 CFR § 314.50 requires comprehensive data for a New Drug Application (NDA). By making the data supply conditional, the changed text creates uncertainty about the completeness of the data. Although neither snippet contains the full text of 21 CFR § 314.50, they both point to the Code of Federal Regulations, indicating the existence of enforceable regulations. The modification introduces ambiguity about BII's responsibility to provide complete data, potentially hindering regulatory compliance and contradicting the requirement of complete data.
